headline: Carnival Fuel Costs Test Profit Gains And 2026 Cruise Demand Strength
summary: Carnival Corporation & (NYSE:CCL) is facing rising cost pressures as global fuel prices climb amid ongoing geopolitical tensions. Industry experts are warning that cruise operators could introduce fuel surcharges for 2026 bookings if energy costs stay elevated. Carnival currently does not hedge its fuel costs, which may leave it more exposed to price shocks than some peers. Carnival Corporation &, trading at around $25.2, sits at an interesting point for investors after a 51.7% gain over...

headline: Here Are the Stocks Rising the Most (and Some Losers)
summary: The market is rallying on hopes of a resumption of energy flows from the Middle East. Here are a few of the stocks moving the most as some of the big moves of recent weeks start to unwind. Going up: ✈️ Shares in Delta Air Lines (DAL), United Airlines (UAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV), which have been walloped by higher fuel prices, are surging premarket.

headline: Oil Prices Bounce; Iran Says Hormuz Opening Hinges On Israel, But S&P 500 Rallies
summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  United Airlines, Carnival and fellow cruise and airline stocks still ranked among top S&P 500 winners in morning trade.  Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.

content.title: Oil Prices Bounce; Iran Says Hormuz Opening Hinges On Israel, But S&P 500 Rallies

Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.
